title: aphyllon corymbosum subsp. mutabile
intro: Aphyllon corymbosum subsp. mutabile, also known as False Unicorn Root, is a plant species belonging to the Asparagaceae family, characterized by its underground rhizome and small, white flowers. Traditionally, False Unicorn Root has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, which are beneficial for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is believed to have health benefits including the relief of menstrual cramps and the stimulation of lactation in nursing mothers, while also serving as an emetic to induce vomiting. False Unicorn Root is typically prepared through decoction or infusion of its dried root, and can also be used as a powder or tincture in herbal remedies.
